extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won his third Vuelta stage from a breakaway, towed to victory by EF teammate CRADDOCK Lawson's sacrificial pace-setting through final kilometers.
tactical_patterns: EF executed textbook leadout where CRADDOCK Lawson drove tempo final 1.5km, preventing attacks while giving CORT ideal second-last wheel position.
DSM tried early peloton control but abandoned chase mid-stage, revealing insufficient team depth to sustain breakaway suppression on consecutive hard days.
SIMMONS Quinn showed late-Vuelta form recovery with aggressive acceleration at 60km-to-go split, signaling U23 riders gain fitness while veterans fade.

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: Third stage win demonstrates repeatable breakaway success pattern when protected by domestique through technical finishes.
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: Sustained 31mph tempo final 1.5km while exhausted on Stage 19 shows exceptional diesel engine capacity late in Grand Tour.
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: positive: Made decisive split at 60km despite hard previous days, suggesting improving form trajectory exiting Vuelta into fall classics.
rider_signals.BAGGIOLI Nicola: neutral: Deckuninck tactics correct but legs insufficient in sprint, indicating team strategy execution better than rider condition Stage 19.
rider_signals.MEINTJES Louis: negative: Involved in crash after moving into top-10 GC, showing vulnerability when fatigued riders navigate technical sections late-race.
